rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 25, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, strict liability, premises owner/contractor liability and loss of consortium and off calendar as to punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, so defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is also precluded from successfully invoking the affirmative negation of an element of plaintiffs¿ claims prong of Aguilar. Assuming without deciding that Ms. Dee¿s declaration is admissible, read in conjunction with Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Metcalf¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to properly make punitive damages an issue as its separate statement did not comply with CRC 3.1350 by failing to list punitive damages as an adjudication issue. Plaintiffs have represented they are withdrawing their strict liability cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
